response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced senior developer with a broad background in web, mobile, and some embedded systems work, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The single hardest requirement—native-level Japanese language proficiency—is entirely absent from his profile (he lists English, Romanian, and basic German). Beyond that critical disqualifier, his C/embedded experience is minimal and incidental rather than a core competency, he has no demonstrated knowledge of network security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal), no L2/L3 switching or Linux kernel modification experience, and no background in LAN-switch product development. His career has been almost entirely oriented toward web platforms, SaaS backends, and TypeScript/Node.js ecosystems.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded systems shows general engineering depth
Documented C and C++ usage at National Instruments and in personal projects (Sound Position Server) confirms at least baseline familiarity with the language
Consistent Linux usage throughout career provides a foundation, even if not at kernel/firmware depth
concerns: Native-level Japanese is a hard listed requirement; the candidate lists no Japanese proficiency whatsoever, making him ineligible regardless of technical fit
No experience with network security domains required by the role (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, Captive Portal, device attack/tamper detection, encryption key exchange)
No evidence of L2/L3 networking, switch firmware, NETCONF/SNMP, chip-vendor SDKs, or any LAN-switch product work; networking background is limited to REST APIs

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or switch security experience
